steps.prompt: Translate: 'Hi, how are you?'
steps.answer: Oi, tudo bem?

steps.prompt: How does a man say 'thank you'?
steps.answer: obrigado

steps.prompt: You need to squeeze past someone on a crowded bus. What do you say?
steps.answer: Com licença.

steps.prompt: What does Ana order?
steps.answer: a coffee with milk (um café com leite)

steps.prompt: How much does it cost?
steps.answer: six reais (seis reais)

steps.prompt: Does the customer speak formally or informally to the barista?
steps.answer: Informally but politely — 'você' plus 'por favor', typical for a café in Brazil.

steps.prompt: Write two sentences: say your name and where you are from (use 'sou de...').
steps.answer: e.g. Oi, eu sou o Alek. Sou dos Estados Unidos.

steps.prompt: Add one more sentence: say where you live now (use 'moro em...').
steps.answer: e.g. Agora moro em São Paulo.

title: Making a friend at a bar

steps.prompt: Greet someone informally and ask where they're from.
steps.answer: E aí, tudo bem? De onde você é?

steps.prompt: They ask what you do. Say you work with computers and ask about them.
steps.answer: Trabalho com computadores. E você, o que você faz?

steps.prompt: Wrap up: say it was nice to meet them and suggest exchanging numbers.
steps.answer: Foi um prazer te conhecer! Vamos trocar número?

steps.prompt: Translate: 'I want to drink water.'
steps.answer: Quero beber água.

steps.prompt: Ask for a coffee and bread, politely.
steps.answer: Um café e um pão, por favor.

steps.prompt: What is the verb for 'to eat'?
steps.answer: comer

title: Ser vs. estar: who you are vs. how you are

steps.prompt: Ser or estar? 'Eu ___ americano.' (permanent identity)
steps.answer: sou (ser for identity/origin: Eu sou americano.)

steps.prompt: Ser or estar? 'O café ___ quente.' (right now)
steps.answer: está (estar for temporary states: O café está quente.)

steps.prompt: Ser or estar? 'A casa ___ na rua principal.' (location of a building)
steps.answer: é or fica — Brazilians usually say 'A casa fica na rua principal'; 'está' is for movable things.

steps.prompt: Translate: 'My friend is tired today.'
steps.answer: Meu amigo está cansado hoje.

steps.prompt: Conjugate: 'Eu ___ português.' (falar)
steps.answer: falo (Eu falo português.)

steps.prompt: Conjugate: 'Você ___ pão de manhã?' (comer)
steps.answer: come (Você come pão de manhã?)

steps.prompt: Translate: 'We drink coffee today.' (use 'a gente' like Brazilians do)
steps.answer: A gente bebe café hoje. (a gente takes the third-person singular verb)

steps.prompt: Translate: 'How much does it cost?'
steps.answer: Quanto custa?

steps.prompt: Translate: 'I didn't understand. Can you speak slower, please?'
steps.answer: Não entendi. Pode falar mais devagar, por favor?

steps.prompt: Translate: 'Excuse me, where is the street?'
steps.answer: Com licença, onde fica a rua?

steps.prompt: Translate: 'Water, please. Thank you!'
steps.answer: Água, por favor. Obrigado! (or Obrigada!)

steps.prompt: What does the receptionist ask the guest for first?
steps.answer: The reservation name and a document (nome da reserva e um documento).

steps.prompt: Is the receptionist formal or informal? What tells you?
steps.answer: Formal — uses 'o senhor / a senhora' and full polite phrases, standard in hotel service.

steps.prompt: What key information should you listen for at check-in?
steps.answer: The room number, the floor, and what time breakfast (café da manhã) is served.

steps.prompt: How does the driver confirm the passenger's identity?
steps.answer: By asking the name on the app ('Você é o/a...?') before starting the ride.

steps.prompt: Is the small talk in the car formal or informal?
steps.answer: Informal — drivers usually chat with 'você', 'e aí', and casual questions about where you're from.

steps.prompt: What phrase asks the driver to stop just ahead?
steps.answer: Pode parar aqui na frente, por favor.

steps.prompt: Text a friend to make plans for Saturday: greet them, suggest an activity, and propose a time.
steps.answer: e.g. Oi! Tudo bem? Vamos na praia sábado? Podemos sair às 10h. Topa?

steps.prompt: Your friend says yes. Reply confirming and say you'll bring water and snacks.
steps.answer: e.g. Fechado! Eu levo água e uns lanches. Até sábado!

steps.prompt: Write a short, professional message to your boss: you woke up sick, you can't come in today, and you'll send the doctor's note.
steps.answer: e.g. Bom dia! Acordei doente hoje e não vou conseguir ir ao trabalho. Vou ao médico e mando o atestado. Peço desculpas pelo transtorno.

steps.prompt: Register check: would 'E aí chefe, tô mal, não vou' be appropriate? Rewrite it if not.
steps.answer: No — too casual for a boss. Better: 'Bom dia. Não estou me sentindo bem e não poderei ir hoje.'

steps.prompt: The waiter says 'Pois não?'. Does that mean 'well, no'? What is its function here?
steps.answer: No — 'Pois não?' is a polite service phrase meaning 'How can I help you?'

steps.prompt: Find the phrase used to ask for the bill and explain the word choice.
steps.answer: 'A conta, por favor.' — 'conta' is the bill; short noun + por favor is the standard polite request.

steps.prompt: Diners use 'a gente' instead of 'nós'. What does this signal about register?
steps.answer: It's the normal spoken-Brazilian 'we' — relaxed but not rude; it takes a singular verb (a gente quer).

steps.prompt: How does the speaker politely stop a stranger on the street?
steps.answer: Com licença... — the standard opener before asking a stranger anything.

steps.prompt: What direction words should you catch in the reply?
steps.answer: direita (right), esquerda (left), reto/direto (straight), quadra (block), esquina (corner).

steps.prompt: The listener didn't catch everything. What can they say?
steps.answer: Desculpa, não entendi. Pode repetir, por favor?

steps.prompt: Write 3-4 sentences describing your neighborhood: where your house is, what is on your street, and one thing you like.
steps.answer: e.g. Moro em um bairro tranquilo. Minha casa fica em uma rua pequena. Na minha rua tem uma padaria e um café. Gosto do bairro porque tem muitos amigos perto.

steps.prompt: Add a sentence with 'perto de' or 'longe de' to say what's near or far.
steps.answer: e.g. Minha casa fica perto do mercado, mas longe da praia.

steps.prompt: You walk into a job interview. Which greeting fits: 'E aí, beleza?' or 'Bom dia, tudo bem?'
steps.answer: 'Bom dia, tudo bem?' — 'E aí, beleza?' is for friends and bars, not interviews.

steps.prompt: A friend of a friend greets you at a bar with 'E aí!'. Give a matching casual reply.
steps.answer: E aí, tudo certo? / Opa, beleza?

steps.prompt: Rank from most formal to most casual: 'Oi, tudo bem?', 'Boa tarde, como vai?', 'Fala, mano!'
steps.answer: 1. Boa tarde, como vai? (formal) 2. Oi, tudo bem? (neutral) 3. Fala, mano! (very casual, friends only)

steps.prompt: When is 'o senhor / a senhora' the safe choice instead of 'você'?
steps.answer: With older people, authority figures (police, doctors, interviewers), and formal service — switch to 'você' only if they invite it.

steps.prompt: What symptom does the customer describe?
steps.answer: A headache / feeling unwell (dor de cabeça; 'não estou me sentindo bem').

steps.prompt: What does the pharmacist ask before recommending something?
steps.answer: Whether the customer has allergies or is taking other medicine (alergia? toma algum remédio?).

steps.prompt: How does the customer ask about dosage?
steps.answer: Como eu tomo? / Quantas vezes por dia?

steps.prompt: Write what you'd say to a pharmacist: greet them, say you have a sore throat since yesterday, and ask what they recommend.
steps.answer: e.g. Boa tarde. Estou com dor de garganta desde ontem. O que o senhor recomenda?

steps.prompt: Follow up: ask how many times per day to take it and how much it costs.
steps.answer: e.g. Quantas vezes por dia eu tomo? E quanto custa?

steps.prompt: Ask the vendor how much the mangoes cost.
steps.answer: Quanto custa a manga? / Quanto tá o quilo da manga?

steps.prompt: The price is 12 reais. Counter-offer politely: say it's a bit expensive and offer 10.
steps.answer: Tá um pouco caro... Faz por dez? / Se eu levar dois quilos, faz dez?

steps.prompt: Close the deal and thank the vendor casually.
steps.answer: Fechado! Valeu, obrigado!

steps.prompt: What documents does the bank clerk ask for?
steps.answer: CPF, an ID document, and proof of address (comprovante de residência).

steps.prompt: Is the clerk formal or informal with the customer?
steps.answer: Formal — 'o senhor / a senhora' and full sentences, standard for banks.

steps.prompt: The customer doesn't understand a banking term. What do they say?
steps.answer: Desculpa, não entendi. O que significa isso?

steps.prompt: Message the restaurant on the delivery app: your order came wrong — you asked for no onion, and you want the juice swapped for water.
steps.answer: e.g. Oi, boa noite! Meu pedido veio errado: pedi sem cebola. E pode trocar o suco por uma água, por favor?

steps.prompt: They apologize and offer to redeliver. Accept and give your address landmark.
steps.answer: e.g. Tudo bem, obrigado! O endereço é o mesmo, perto da padaria da rua principal.

steps.prompt: What two plan options does the attendant present?
steps.answer: A monthly plan (plano mensal) and a cheaper annual plan (plano anual) with a commitment (fidelidade).

steps.prompt: What does the customer negotiate?
steps.answer: Waiving the enrollment fee (taxa de matrícula) / a discount for paying upfront.

steps.prompt: Is the attendant's tone formal or informal? Why does that fit?
steps.answer: Friendly-informal ('você', upbeat sales talk) — gyms in Brazil use casual, persuasive register.

steps.prompt: A colleague invites you to a barbecue Sunday, but you can't go. Decline warmly: thank them, give a brief reason, and suggest another time.
steps.answer: e.g. Poxa, obrigado pelo convite! Infelizmente não vou poder — já tenho um compromisso no domingo. Vamos marcar outro dia? Fica pra próxima!

steps.prompt: What softeners make a refusal sound friendly in Brazilian Portuguese?
steps.answer: 'Poxa', 'infelizmente', 'que pena', and always 'fica pra próxima' — a flat 'não posso' alone sounds cold.

title: Valeu, beleza, cara: everyday slang and where it's safe

steps.prompt: What does 'valeu' mean and where can you use it?
steps.answer: Casual 'thanks' — fine with friends, drivers, vendors; in a formal email or with your boss's boss, use 'obrigado/obrigada'.

steps.prompt: 'Beleza?' as a greeting and 'beleza' as an answer — what do they each mean?
steps.answer: As a greeting: 'all good?'. As a reply: 'okay / deal'. Both casual — don't open a job interview with 'Beleza?'.

steps.prompt: Your bank manager helped you. Which is the safe thanks: 'Valeu, mano!' or 'Muito obrigado pela ajuda'?
steps.answer: 'Muito obrigado pela ajuda' — 'mano' is for close friends and reads disrespectful in formal service settings.

steps.prompt: What does 'cara' mean in 'Esse cara é meu amigo'?
steps.answer: 'Guy/dude' — informal but common; as a direct address ('Cara, ...') it's friends-only.

steps.prompt: What problem does the customer report?
steps.answer: A wrong charge / a service that stopped working (uma cobrança errada; o serviço parou de funcionar).

steps.prompt: What does the attendant ask for to locate the account?
steps.answer: CPF or the customer/order number (número do pedido, número do cliente).

steps.prompt: How does the customer stay polite while insisting on a resolution?
steps.answer: Uses 'por favor', 'eu entendo, mas...' and asks for a protocol number (número de protocolo) — firm but courteous.

steps.prompt: Write a polite message to your landlord: the kitchen sink has been leaking for a week, and you'd like a repair scheduled this week.
steps.answer: e.g. Boa tarde, tudo bem? Estou escrevendo porque a pia da cozinha está vazando há uma semana. O senhor poderia agendar um reparo ainda esta semana, por favor? Fico no aguardo. Obrigado!

steps.prompt: Which closing fits a landlord message: 'Falou, até!' or 'Fico no aguardo, obrigado'?
steps.answer: 'Fico no aguardo, obrigado' — polite-neutral; 'Falou' is buddies-only slang.

title: Striking up conversation at a bar

steps.prompt: Open casually: comment on the music or the place and introduce yourself.
steps.answer: e.g. E aí, curtindo o som? Eu sou o Alek, prazer.

steps.prompt: Offer to buy them a drink without being pushy.
steps.answer: Posso te pagar uma bebida? O que você tá bebendo?

steps.prompt: They mention loving live music. React and keep the conversation going.
steps.answer: Sério? Eu também! Você conhece algum lugar bom com música ao vivo por aqui?

steps.prompt: Write a friendly, specific opener for a match whose profile shows they love cooking and the beach — reference the profile and ask a question.
steps.answer: e.g. Oi! Vi que você adora cozinhar — qual é o teu prato favorito de fazer? Eu tô aprendendo, mas por enquanto só faço café bem. haha

steps.prompt: They reply well. Suggest meeting for a coffee, keeping it low-pressure.
steps.answer: e.g. Adorei conversar contigo! Topa tomar um café qualquer dia dessa semana? Conheço um lugar ótimo.

steps.prompt: Your date arrives. Greet them warmly and say they look great.
steps.answer: Oi! Que bom te ver. Você tá ótima! (or: Você tá ótimo!)

steps.prompt: Ask about their week and what they like to do on weekends.
steps.answer: Como foi tua semana? O que você gosta de fazer no fim de semana?

steps.prompt: End the date suggesting you'd like to see them again.
steps.answer: Adorei a noite. Vamos repetir? Me manda mensagem quando chegar em casa.

steps.prompt: What three questions does the immigration officer ask?
steps.answer: Purpose of the trip (motivo da viagem), length of stay (quanto tempo vai ficar), and where the traveler will stay (onde vai ficar).

steps.prompt: Should the traveler answer with 'o senhor/a senhora' or 'você'? Why?
steps.answer: 'O senhor / a senhora' — immigration officers are authority figures; formal register is the safe choice.

steps.prompt: The traveler didn't understand a question. What's the right move?
steps.answer: Say 'Desculpa, não entendi. Pode repetir mais devagar, por favor?' — never guess an answer at immigration.

title: What did you do last weekend? (perfeito practice)

steps.prompt: Write 4 sentences about last weekend using the pretérito perfeito: where you went, what you ate, who you saw, and what you did at home.
steps.answer: e.g. No sábado fui à praia com amigos. Comemos peixe em um restaurante na rua da praia. No domingo vi minha amiga Ana e a gente falou por horas. À noite fiquei em casa e bebi um café.

steps.prompt: Convert to perfeito: 'Eu como pão' → last Saturday; 'Eu falo com meu amigo' → yesterday.
steps.answer: No sábado eu comi pão. Ontem eu falei com meu amigo.

steps.prompt: Greet the interviewer formally and thank them for the opportunity.
steps.answer: Bom dia! Muito prazer. Obrigado pela oportunidade de estar aqui.

steps.prompt: They ask 'Fale um pouco sobre você.' Give a 2-sentence professional self-intro.
steps.answer: Eu sou desenvolvedor e trabalho com tecnologia há cinco anos. Gosto de resolver problemas e de trabalhar em equipe.

steps.prompt: Ask one good question at the end and close politely.
steps.answer: Como é o dia a dia da equipe? ... Muito obrigado pelo seu tempo, foi um prazer.

steps.prompt: How do the friends react when their team scores?
steps.answer: They shout 'Gol!' / 'É gol!' and celebrate ('Aí sim!', 'Golaço!').

steps.prompt: Is the language formal or informal? Name two markers.
steps.answer: Very informal — slang like 'cara', 'mano', 'tá', dropped syllables, and exclamations throughout.

steps.prompt: What does someone say to complain about the referee?
steps.answer: Something like 'Esse juiz tá de brincadeira!' — the referee (juiz/árbitro) is always fair game for complaints.
